parameters.content:
## AI Receipt Expense Memory

### How it works

This workflow watches Gmail for receipt emails, normalizes the email content, downloads receipt attachments, and checks configured expense memory stores to avoid processing duplicates. For new receipts, it uses OpenAI to extract expense details from email text first. If the email body has little/no usable text and a receipt image attachment is available, it falls back to OpenAI image OCR using the same JSON schema. It then optionally sends Slack and Microsoft Teams alerts, writes the expense record to Google Sheets and/or Airtable, and finishes with a final receipt-processing response.

### Setup steps

- Connect Gmail credentials and configure the Gmail trigger to match receipt emails or labels. Leave attachment download enabled for OCR fallback.
- Edit the setup/configuration code node with receipt settings, duplicate-check options, OCR fallback settings, logging toggles, sheet/base identifiers, alert toggles, and webhook/channel details.
- Connect OpenAI credentials and review the receipt extraction/OCR prompt and model settings.
- If Slack alerts are enabled, connect Slack credentials and set the destination channel.
- If Teams alerts are enabled, provide a valid Microsoft Teams incoming webhook URL.
- If Google Sheets logging or lookup is enabled, connect Google Sheets credentials and configure the spreadsheet, sheet, and column mapping.
- If Airtable logging or lookup is enabled, connect Airtable credentials and configure the base, table, and field mapping.

### Customization

Adjust the duplicate matching rules, OCR threshold, extracted receipt fields, alert message formatting, and which memory/log destinations are enabled in the configuration and code nodes.

## Gmail receipt intake

Starts the workflow when a receipt-related email arrives in Gmail.

## Configure and normalize email

Loads user-defined receipt-processing settings and converts the Gmail payload into a consistent structure for downstream checks.

## Airtable memory lookup

Branches based on whether Airtable memory is enabled, reads prior expense records when enabled, or builds a skip bundle when disabled.

## Sheets memory lookup

Branches based on whether Google Sheets memory is enabled, retrieves existing expense rows when enabled, or passes through the prior lookup bundle when disabled.

## Duplicate receipt gate

Compares the normalized receipt against lookup rows and only allows new receipts to continue to extraction.

## Extract receipt details

Uses OpenAI to parse receipt email text. If the body has little usable text and an image attachment is available, OpenAI image OCR extracts the same receipt JSON fields before analysis and alert formatting.

## Slack receipt alert

Conditionally posts the prepared receipt alert to Slack, then records whether the Slack notification was sent or skipped.

## Teams receipt alert

Conditionally sends the receipt alert to Microsoft Teams through a webhook and records the sent or skipped status.

## Build log record

Combines extracted receipt data and notification status into the expense record that will be written to enabled logs.

## Sheets write decision

Checks whether the final expense record should be written to Google Sheets.

## Write Sheets expense

Appends the expense to Google Sheets when enabled, confirms the write, or records that the Sheets write was skipped.

## Airtable write decision

Checks whether the final expense record should also be written to Airtable.

## Write Airtable expense

Creates the expense record in Airtable when enabled, confirms the write, or records that the Airtable write was skipped.

## Finalize receipt result

Produces the final response summarizing the receipt extraction, alert delivery, duplicate status, and logging outcomes.

parameters.jsCode:
const inputItem = $input.first();
return [{ json: inputItem.json, binary: inputItem.binary || {} }];

values.content: You extract receipt and invoice data for expense tracking. Return ONLY valid JSON with keys: isReceipt (boolean), extractionStatus (extracted|not_receipt|failed), failureReason (string), merchant, purchaseDate, total, currency, category, taxDeductible, taxCategory, paymentMethod, lastFour, items, warrantyItems, returnDeadline, warrantyEndDate, aiSummary, confidence, needsReview, notes. If the email is not a receipt/invoice/purchase confirmation, set isReceipt=false and extractionStatus=not_receipt. Use ISO dates when possible. confidence is 0-100. needsReview true when totals conflict, merchant is unclear, or total is missing.

parameters.jsCode:
const item = $input.first().json;
return [{ json: { ...item, alertSent: 'yes' } }];

const item = $input.first().json;
return [{ json: { ...item, alertSent: item.alertSent || 'no' } }];

const item = $input.first().json;
return [{ json: { ...item, alertSent: 'yes' } }];

const item = $input.first().json;
return [{ json: { ...item, alertSent: item.alertSent || 'no' } }];

const item = $input.first().json;
return [
  {
    json: {
      expenseLog: item.expenseLog,
      useSheetsLog: item.useSheetsLog,
      useAirtableLog: item.useAirtableLog,
      googleSheetId: item.googleSheetId,
      sheetsTabName: item.sheetsTabName,
      airtableBaseId: item.airtableBaseId,
      expensesTableName: item.expensesTableName,
      'Received At': item.receivedAt,
      Source: item.source,
      'Message ID': item.messageId,
      'Receipt Hash': item.receiptHash,
      Merchant: item.merchant,
      'Purchase Date': item.purchaseDate,
      Total: item.total,
      Currency: item.currency,
      Category: item.category,
      'Tax Deductible': item.taxDeductible ? 'yes' : 'no',
      'Tax Category': item.taxCategory,
      'Payment Method': item.paymentMethod,
      'Last Four': item.lastFour,
      Items: item.items,
      'Warranty Items': item.warrantyItems,
      'Return Deadline': item.returnDeadline,
      'Warranty End Date': item.warrantyEndDate,
      'AI Summary': item.aiSummary,
      'Extraction Source': item.extractionSource,
      'Is Receipt': item.isReceipt ? 'yes' : 'no',
      'Extraction Status': item.extractionStatus,
      'Extraction Failure Reason': item.extractionFailureReason,
      Confidence: item.confidence,
      'Needs Review': item.needsReview ? 'yes' : 'no',
      'High Value': item.highValue ? 'yes' : 'no',
      'Alert Sent': item.alertSent || 'no',
      Status:
        item.route === 'review'
          ? 'Needs Review'
          : item.route === 'high_value'
            ? 'High Value'
            : item.route === 'warranty'
              ? 'Warranty / Return'
              : 'Logged',
      'Original Subject': item.originalSubject,
      'From Email': item.fromEmail,
      Notes: item.notes,
      'Raw Text Snippet': item.rawTextSnippet,
      _testScenario: item._testScenario || '',
    },
  },
];

const prior = $('Create Expense Log').first().json;
return [{ json: { ...prior, sheetsLogged: 'yes' } }];

const item = $input.first().json;
return [{ json: { ...item, sheetsLogged: 'no' } }];

const prior = $('Check Airtable Log Requirement').first().json;
return [{ json: { ...prior, airtableLogged: 'yes' } }];

const item = $input.first().json;
return [{ json: { ...item, airtableLogged: 'no' } }];
